page: OVHcloud l4-180 is a GPU-accelerated server belonging to the Cloud GPU family, powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9454 processor with 45 dedicated physical cores running at 2.75 GHz, with one thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 180.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core, alongside 400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Acceleration is driven by two NVIDIA L4 GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering a combined 48 GB of VRAM. Network connectivity is supported by a 16 Gbps baseline bandwidth, and the system includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier performance. The AMD EPYC processor achieves top 10% placement in both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests, supported by strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench scores. Memory performance is notable, with PassMark memory scores ranking in the top 10% and small-block read-only memory bandwidth placing in the top 25%. The dual NVIDIA L4 GPUs deliver top-tier LLM inference speeds, achieving top 10% performance for prompt processing and text generation across small 135M and medium 7B models, as well as top 10% prompt processing for large 70B models.

From a resource density perspective, the l4-180 offers a highly cost-efficient profile for GPU-intensive workloads, balancing dedicated AMD EPYC compute cores with dual Ada Lovelace accelerators. While large-block uncached memory read bandwidth and single-threaded compression tasks perform at average levels, the server's massive parallel processing capabilities and high-speed NVMe storage mitigate these bottlenecks for target applications. This configuration is highly suited for machine learning workloads, including LLM inference, data science pipelines, ray tracing, and database operations.
description: OVHcloud l4-180 is a GPU-accelerated server designed for compute-intensive and machine learning workloads. Powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9454 processor, it features 45 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.75 GHz, paired with 180.0 GB of system memory and 400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Graphics and parallel processing are driven by two NVIDIA L4 Ada Lovelace GPUs, providing a total of 48 GB VRAM. The server delivers top-tier performance in CPU stress tests and LLM inference benchmarks, particularly in prompt processing and text generation. With a 16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, the l4-180 represents a highly capable platform for artificial intelligence, large-scale database operations, and complex rendering tasks.
og_description: OVHcloud l4-180 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 45 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 180 GB RAM, 400 GB NVMe storage, and dual NVIDIA L4 GPUs with 48 GB total VRAM for high-performance AI and database workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud l4-180 is a KVM-based GPU server with 45 AMD EPYC cores, 180 GB RAM, and dual NVIDIA L4 GPUs, optimized for LLM inference and data science.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC server equipped with dual NVIDIA L4 GPUs and NVMe storage for accelerated machine learning and parallel computing.
bullet_points: Powered by 45 dedicated AMD EPYC 9454 physical cores running at 2.75 GHz.
Equipped with dual NVIDIA L4 Ada Lovelace GPUs providing 48 GB total VRAM.
Includes 180 GB of system memory and 400 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.
Delivers top-tier LLM inference speeds and CPU multi-core benchmark performance.
Features 16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and one public IPv4 address.
Optimized for machine learning inference, data science, and database operations.
